reviews.review_description:
Taiko Frenzy is a fantastic Taiko rhythm game that truly captures the power and spirit of Japanese drumming. The gameplay offers great variety, from striking the drumhead to hitting the rim, rapid sequences, and rhythm changes that keep each track engaging. The execution is flawless and highly precise—you almost feel like a real taiko drum is right in front of you.

The environments are beautiful and immersive, perfectly matching the intensity and flow of the music. The progression system is especially rewarding, with unlockable drum skins, new sticks, different drums, and adorable charms for your bachi that you’ll absolutely want to collect. The mix of traditional and modern music styles adds strong replayability and keeps sessions fresh.

This is a true hidden gem that somehow flew under the radar for far too long. If you enjoy rhythm games or want an authentic, energetic drumming experience in VR, Taiko Frenzy is a must-discover.

Super cool if you enjoy playing instruments like myself :) Although I enjoyed a few songs my only issue was that I kept wishing that there were popular song that I LOVE. Having a playlist like Today’s Top Hits? That’d be cool!

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to review our game. Did you test the community songs feature of Taiko Frenzy ? We have made tools available for the community to play their own custom songs, feel free to test it out.

reviews.review_description: I used to have the old PlayStation Taiko Drum Master (physical drum and sticks) years ago.. I really missed playing that game. When I saw this game I had to buy it.. So far really liking the game!
Im a music major. This game helped me improve my coordination and tempo in a really fun way. It's also very relaxing because it doesn't scream at you when you make a mistake. I highly recommend.
I was looking for a Taiko VR game and this did not disappoint. Hoping for more content and community growth
Really fun rhythm game, plenty of  things to get to grips with like the side hits. Wonderful game, very refreshing.

reviews.review_title: Like the Arcade Game Come to Life!
reviews.review_description:
It's like a huge version of the Arcade Game Taiko No Tatsujin come to life. It's these kinds of things that make VR so awesome. 

4 stars for me means I felt it was worth the purchase and I recommend it.

To make 5 stars they'd have to blow my socks off. It's not at that level. 

More environments and more songs would help. I wish it had a mode with actual traditional taiko songs so I could learn to actually play a real song and not just make noise lol (Community songs may make that possible).

This is honestly my favourite game on vr, as the gameplay is super clean + feels realistic. Ive tried all sorts of rhythm games on quest, but this is the most “rhythmic” of the ones I’ve tried- it feels like playing the drums and a good understanding of the rhythms you’re playing comes in handy. From a Taiko no Tatsujin player, it reminds me of playing in the actual arcade, and the additional mechanics like accent notes feel like nice additions for vr.
The entire atmosphere also adds to the gameplay experience in a manner that should not be understated: the sakura theme is beautiful makes grabbing sakura multipliers feel incredibly satisfying. 
Overall great, and the support for custom songs is appreciated and makes it possible to always have fresh gameplay, though the included OST honestly has some bangers too.

I know there is an edit I can DIY and there are some community songs.  TBH I really tried to use the editor to create some songs of my own but it is way harder than I thought (may be I am just lacking the skill to do so) When I tried it everything is off it is as if I just randomly add notes here and there LOL.  Please add more songs DLC and with reasonable price I will buy them all.  Love your game and nope I have not mastered all the existing songs yet but the ADHD inside me just wanna see more songs at least like Taikonotatsujin ;)

Thoroughly enjoying this. I've always loved rhythm games and this is a really good one. It's pretty tough but with practice I'm definitely getting better and the soundtrack is great. Makes a nice change from the usual rock or techno in most rhythm games

reviews.review_title: Well executed Taiko drum rhythm game
reviews.review_description: The developers really put some nice touches into this rhythm game! The Japanese style of the songs are wonderful and it is really fun to play. If you are a fan of the original Taiko non Tatsujin (a classic Japanese arcade game also on Switch) then you should ABSOLUTELY get this one!

reviews.review_title: A solid start, but needs some work
reviews.review_description:
Actually wanted to go way more in-depth with this review, but character limit on the Meta store is forcing me to keep myself short.

I think this game is a good start. What's already here is quite solid, but I also have some major struggles with it.

I'm a seasoned player of Taiko no Tatsujin, and despite consistently clearing some 9 star Oni songs in there, I'm struggling even on the lowest difficulties in Taiko Frenzy. You wouldn't expect this with how similar the two games are.

I think my main issue here is the presentation. Having four different hittable note positions represented by just two lanes confuses my brain so much. I think color-coding is not a great way to differentiate the four positions. It works in Taiko no Tatsujin, because it doesn't care between left and right hits and also uses a different layout, but here, it messes with me so hard.

I think there's actually an easy fix for this problem: Just have four lanes instead, at least as an option. That way you just need to hit the drum exactly where the notes are appearing on the screen, which I think is pretty easy for the brain to work out (based on my recent experience getting into Ragnarök, where I could immediately perform decently on even the medium difficulty).

Alternatively, a "beginner mode" with just a single lane and no discrimination between left and right notes could help.

Presentation aside, I think this game's soundtrack isn't quite there yet. I'm generally glad it's going with a traditional Japanese style. I like this kind of music, and it makes the game quite unique and just fits its aesthetic and gameplay very well. That being said, I think it's lacking some truely memorable songs. None of the current ones have that "oomph" that I've come to appreciate so much about the Taiko no Tatsujin soundtrack. Nothing that makes me want to keep coming back yet.

I'll keep observing this game. With just a little more work, it could become one of my favorite VR titles, but not quite yet.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, thank you for taking the time to write such a detailed feedback on Taiko Frenzy.
Taiko no Tatsujin is definitely one of our inspirations for this game, but our objective is not to create a clone of it.
We have iterated a lot to find our own gameplay/presentation that feels good in VR. With a little bit of practice, we are confident that you should do just fine and enjoy it.
That being said, we think of implementing other game modes in the future without lateralization, we just need to find the best way to do it.
Stay tuned and feel free to come say hello on our Discord ;)

reviews.review_title: Great game for rhythm game fans!
reviews.review_description: If you like rhythm games I think this is a must-buy. The concept is simple enough to get you started but to get really good is very challenging.

This game is great. I can't believe it isn't more popular. Maybe I'm speaking slightly biased but if you love music and you have a gift and a talent for it and do you like playing percussion, you will love it! By the way, I'm also a big fan of the traditional Oriental music

It's a very good simulation game, but there are a few points that could be improved to make the experience even better. Firstly, I find it unfortunate that the drumsticks go through the drum when hit, diminishing the realistic aspect of the gameplay. Additionally, the volume of the drum is barely audible, or even nonexistent on certain music tracks, even after adjusting the audio settings. Increasing the drum volume would be beneficial for a more immersive sound experience.

Enriching the game by adding different environments would be an excellent idea. For example, introducing a nighttime environment could add a touch of magic to the gaming experience. Furthermore, the ability to have drums of different colors could add variety and customization to the game.

Regarding the price, offering the pack at $5 instead of $11.99 could attract more players and raise awareness of the product. Overall, the game is quite good and provides a nice simulation experience, but these improvements could make it even more appealing.

I just went thru the tutorial and understood immediately what is there to do. After that, I could just enjoy the nice music and drum in rhythm to it. It is not just drumming, you got to pull off some special moves as well, so it needs a bit of concentration and coordination. Put these things together and you got a really satisfactory experience in a relaxing environment. I can see myself to go and drum a bit after a hard days at work.  Keep up the good work!

First, thank you developers for this fun and challenging VR game, I enjoyed it. 
The environment and the music are all good and relaxing to play
Yes, more different colorful environment please, more gameplay because it is just a demo now.  
Will buy the full game if more gameplay to come .  Yes recommend this game for everyone  to enjoy.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your kind words, we'll keep working hard on the game until the full release!  Feel free to join our Discord server if you want to make suggestions to improve the game.

reviews.review_description:
One of the more polished music games.  It could use some different environments but its early access so that may come

One thing I like to see in music games is physical movement which there isnt much here but the mechanic where you raise one arm to trigger the combo is pretty satisfying.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your kind review, please join our Discord server if you want to make any suggestion to improve the game. We would be very interested to understand better what you mean by physical movement.

reviews.review_description:
i have some ideas though.
why can't i use the right stick to hit notes on the left and vice versa? that would be so much more freeing in terms of play style!

This is a hidden gem of the Quest’s library of rhythm games. Fun gameplay reminiscent of Taiko no Tatsujin with cool cosmetics to unlock. Custom song functionality is great but I wish the community to make them was larger and more centralised. Still a great buy!
